question.text: Which of the following is NOT an exemption from the mandatory PAN requirement in securities market?

answer.text: Senior citizens above 70 years of age
Transactions on behalf of Central/State Government
Investors residing in the state of Sikkim
SIP of Mutual Funds up to Rs. 50,000 per year

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The PAN exemptions are limited to: (i) Central/State Government transactions and court-appointed officials, (ii) investors in Sikkim, (iii) UN entities/multilateral agencies exempt from taxes, and (iv) MF SIP up to Rs. 50,000/year. Senior citizens above 70 are NOT exempt from PAN requirements.
**The Trap:** Senior citizens may have simplified KYC in some contexts but are NOT exempt from PAN in securities markets. The Sikkim exemption is a unique historical provision that many candidates forget.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM V-A, the SIP exemption up to Rs. 50,000/year is practically important for mutual fund distributors. The Sikkim exemption is a specific regulatory fact tested in this exam.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Memorize the four PAN exemptions: Government, Sikkim residents, UN entities, and MF SIP up to Rs. 50,000. Every other client category needs PAN. If a client claims exemption, verify they actually fall in one of these four categories.
